strengths: describe.skipIf(!process.env.RUN_LLM_IT) gate is the idiomatic Vitest skip — dry run skips cleanly and exits 0 with zero network calls (verified: 1 test, 1 skipped)

executeCard spy records each systemPrompt then delegates to the REAL executeCard from llm.js (pipeline.integration.test.ts:30-41) — no mockResolvedValue, so the crown-jewel no-leak assertion runs against actually-rendered prompts
Spy parameter order (systemPrompt, intent, previousOutputs, reasoning, onToken) matches the AgentBoardServiceDeps.executeCard signature exactly (service.ts:105-111)
No-leak regex /\{[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\}/ matches the spec verbatim and is applied to both prompts and outputs (pipeline.integration.test.ts:78-86)
In-memory DI doubles for getBoard/getColumns/insertOutput/insertCard/updateBoard/publishEvent — no Postgres or Redis connection anywhere in the file
Structural-invariant assertions only (5 outputs, card_index 0..4, boardStatus 'done', trim().length > 0) — no exact LLM text assertions, safe for non-deterministic output
mockColumns built from the live getTemplate('research-report') definition, keeping the test in sync with the real 5-column template
Commit fcc12e7 uses correct conventional-commit message: 'test(agent): add opt-in headless live-LLM integration test for runPipeline'

issues.description: captured.events is collected via the publishEvent double but never asserted on. Harmless and not a QUALITY BAR requirement, but it is unused captured state that could be dropped or used to assert SSE per-card transitions.
